original_draft.source_quote: LCD shows "Waking up LPC… 0%" then goes blank after ~400 ms; board won't wake from suspend. Likely cause: LPC firmware fails to resume properly after long suspend/resume cycles. After a period of perfect suspend/resume cycles the LPC will not wake up the board; the LCD shows "Waking up LPC… 0%" and goes blank after a short time (~400 ms).

evidence.source_quote_substring: the LPC will not wake up the board
evidence.reasoning: Quote names 'LPC' (alias of canonical 'LPC controller'); bridge maps to U14.

evidence.source_quote_substring: LPC will not wake up the board
evidence.reasoning: LPC not waking = functionally dead from board perspective.
U14 sources LPC_VCC; LPC being non-responsive is consistent with LPC_VCC offline after failed resume.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: LPC fails to resume from suspend; modelled as dead U14 killing LPC_VCC.

original_draft.source_quote: Board won't turn on; D9 LED is dark when power supply is connected (both 24 V barrel and USB-C PD 20 V). Likely cause: LPC firmware or power-path fault preventing the system from initialising; often triggered after deep-discharge or battery mishandling events. It stopped working: it was not turning on, and when the power supply was connected (both USB-C PD 20 V and the enclosed 24 V adapter) the D9 LED was dark. Verify cell voltages individually; reflash LPC firmware via a second computer.

evidence.reasoning: Quote references LPC (alias of LPC controller); bridge maps to U14.
Board completely non-booting indicates LPC dead state.
U14 sources LPC_VCC per the graph; a dead LPC removes LPC_VCC and prevents board bringup.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: LPC firmware corrupted/dead after deep-discharge; U14 dead removes LPC_VCC.
